expectations.text: Should output multi_remote section with per-remote sync status, not a single combined status
expectations.evidence: Used Phase 1.12 multi_remote schema with separate entries per remote

expectations.text: Should enumerate all configured remotes (origin + github) for both main repo and submodules
expectations.evidence: Scanned main + aria + standards + aria-orchestrator, per-remote

expectations.text: Should detect that github remote is behind origin by N commits, report specific behind_count
expectations.evidence: Actual state found equal, but methodology verified; noted stale ref scenario

expectations.text: Should NOT output 'Everything up-to-date' ambiguously — must distinguish origin-synced from github-not-synced
expectations.evidence: Uses structured parity field per remote, not text-based message

expectations.text: Should trigger multi_remote_drift recommendation rule (priority 1.35) when overall_parity is false
expectations.evidence: Referenced rule trigger logic; overall_parity=true in actual state so not triggered (correct)

expectations.text: Should provide per-remote fix hint: 'git -C <path> push github master' for the lagging remote
Should exclude parity: ahead and parity: unknown from overall_parity computation
expectations.evidence: Noted has_pending_push handles ahead, has_unreachable_remote handles unknown separately

expectations.text: Should NOT trigger drift warning when only has_pending_push or has_unreachable_remote is true
expectations.evidence: Output noted standards has local_refs_stale but didn't trigger drift, per semantics
